evals.prompt: How do I protect NestJS routes with JWT authentication?
evals.expected_output: Create a guard implementing CanActivate. In canActivate(), extract the token from the Authorization header (split(' ')[1]), verify it with JwtService.verify(), attach the payload to request['user'], and return true. Throw UnauthorizedException for missing or invalid tokens. Apply with @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard) at controller or method level.

evals.prompt: How do I automatically validate request bodies in NestJS?
evals.expected_output: Enable the global ValidationPipe in main.ts with whitelist: true and transform: true. Then decorate DTO class properties with class-validator decorators: @IsEmail(), @IsString(), @MinLength(8), @IsOptional(). The pipe validates incoming @Body() automatically and returns 400 with field error messages for invalid input. transform: true converts plain objects to DTO class instances.

evals.prompt: How do I return consistent error responses across all NestJS routes?
evals.expected_output: Create a global exception filter with @Catch(HttpException) implementing ExceptionFilter. In catch(), read exception.getStatus() and exception.getResponse(), then write a consistent JSON shape to the response. Register with app.useGlobalFilters(). Also add a catch-all @Catch() filter for unexpected errors that returns 500 — register it first (least specific filter first).
